XOnlineCompetitionTopologySingleElimination

Searches the topology of a single-elimination competition.

HRESULT XOnlineCompetitionTopologySingleElimination(
    DWORD dwTemplate,
    ULONGLONG qwCompetitionID,
    DWORD dwOriginEventTopologyID,
    DWORD dwRoundsForward,
    DWORD dwRoundsBackward,
    DWORD dwTopWidth,
    DWORD dwNumResultAttributeSpecs,
    const XONLINE_ATTRIBUTE_SPEC *pResultAttributeSpecs,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwTemplate
[in] ID of the data set associated with the competition.
qwCompetitionID
[in] Unique ID for the competition.
dwOriginEventTopologyID
[in] Topology ID of the focal event. For more information about competition events and topologies, see XOnlineCompetitionTopology.
dwRoundsForward
[in] Number of rounds subsequent to dwOriginEventTopologyID to retrieve.
dwRoundsBackward
[in] Number of rounds prior to dwOriginEventTopologyID to retrieve.
dwTopWidth
[in] Number of events in the lowest round to be returned.
dwNumResultAttributeSpecs
[in] Number of attribute specifiers in the pResultAttributeSpecs parameter.
pResultAttributeSpecs
[in] Array of XONLINE_ATTRIBUTE_SPEC structures containing title-specific attributes associated with the competition. This array must start with an integer attribute whose type is set to XONLINE_QUERY_ENTITY_ID. It is followed by the list and size of the return values configured in the Topology query in XLAST.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle of an event (not a game event) you want to associate with this task. The event is fired after the task completes. If you do not want to associate an event with this task, set this parameter to NULL.
phTask
[out] Address of an XONLINETASK_HANDLE variable that receives the handle for the task created by this function.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise. The error codes can include the following.

Error Code Description
XONLINE_E_QUERY_SPEC_COUNT_MISMATCH The number of attributes specified in pResultAttributeSpecs does not match the list of return values on the server, which was configured by XLAST.
XONLINE_E_QUERY_ATTRIBUTE_TOO_LONG A string or BLOB (binary block of data) type attribute listed in pSearchAttributes or pResultAttributeSpecs specifies a length that is smaller than the data stored on the server.
XONLINE_E_QUERY_DATASET_NOT_FOUND The specified template does not correspond to a template configured in XLAST.

Remarks

Single-elimination competitions are often represented as trees, such as the one in the following illustration. In this picture, the players are represented by the letters A, B, C, and so on. Each layer of the tree is a single round. A round consists of one or more events, which could also be called matches. A unique ID is assigned to each node in the tree, as shown in the figure below. Every node in the tree has a unique ID, called a topology ID, as shown to the right of the pair of players participating in the event.

Topology ID numbers are hexadecimal numbers that are eight digits (four bytes) long. The most-significant two bytes contain the round number, which starts with 0. The least-significant two bytes contain the event number, also starting with 0. The figure shows that nodes in the first round are numbered 0x00000000, 0x00000001, and so forth. Nodes in the second round are numbered 0x00010000, 0x00010001, and so on. The section of the tree that this function returns is relative to the origin event, whose topology ID number is passed into this function through the qwOriginEventTopologyID parameter.

The dwRoundsForward and dwRoundsBackward parameters specify the number of rounds to search before and after the origin. For example, in the search illustrated by the following figure, the origin event is indicated by the red star. The events between the vertical red lines are the rounds forward and backward that are included in the search. The events between the blue horizontal lines specify the width of the search, which is passed to this function through the dwTopWidth parameter.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

XOnlineCompetitionTopology, XOnlineCompetitionTopologySingleEliminationGetResults, TOPOLOGY_ID
